title: Kernel bootstrap for this Codex session. Read and execute the repository-authoritative prompt at: /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md Run id: adhoc:MacBook-Pro-M5-5:kernel-workspace:25971:run:20260404T090520:52378 Bootstrap receipt path: /Users/masayuki/.config/ker...
summary_text: - **Critical: receipt split-brain for this run.** The canonical in-repo receipt is missing at [repo bootstrap receipt](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.fugue/kernel-state/bootstrap-receipts/adhoc_MacBook-Pro-M5-5_kernel-workspace_25971_run_20260404T090520_52378.json), while a valid receipt exists at [home bootstrap receipt](/Users/masayuki/.config/kernel/bootstrap-receipts/adhoc_MacBook-Pro-M5-5_kernel-workspace_25971_run_20260404T090520_52378.json). With current sandbox/write context, health resolves to repo-local state root (fallback behavior in [kernel-state-paths.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-state-paths.sh#L49)), so this run is judged `invalid` despite an external receipt. - **High: bootstrap receipt can overstate active lanes.** Receipt write accepts caller-provided lane/model/label fields without validating live processes ([kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh#L49)), and launch records that receipt before any GLM/specialist execution evidence exists ([codex-kernel-guard.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh#L532)). - **High: required-voice enforcement is “at least one success per provider,” not active-lane proof.** Health checks provider success counters and receipt flags ([kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh#L151)), but does not verify 6 concurrent lanes or ongoing lane liveness except tmux when scheduler is `running`/`continuity_degraded` ([kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh#L225)). This allows false-positive “healthy” states after minimal serialized evidence. - **Medium: compact scheduler metadata is env-overridable and currently corrupted in this run.** Compact update trusts `KERNEL_SCHEDULER_STATE`/`KERNEL_SCHEDULER_REASON` if present ([kernel-compact-artifact.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh#L564)); this run’s compact artifact has scheduler fields set to a workspace receipt path in [run compact artifact](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.fugue/kernel-state/compact/adhoc_MacBook-Pro-M5-5_kernel-workspace_25971_run_20260404T090520_52378.json), indicating a false-status risk in operator surfaces. - **Medium: degraded transition can be declared before degraded shape is evidenced.** `glm-fail` flips ledger state to `degraded-allowed` purely from GLM failure count ([codex-kernel-guard.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh#L751)); specialist evidence is enforced later by health/phase-gate, so interim status can be misleading. - **Medium: health checks mutate state by default.** `kernel-runtime-health.sh` writes ledger transitions on status checks ([kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh#L242)), so observational checks can overwrite reasons/state and mask sequencing/debug history.
